expectations.text: Output contains all four sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
expectations.evidence: Output has '## Mission', '## Goals', '## Premises', and '## Constraints' as distinct labeled sections.

expectations.text: Mission captures the core intent (replace legacy Access reporting tool for finance) — not just a list of features
expectations.evidence: Mission: 'Replace the legacy Microsoft Access-based internal reporting tool (circa 2009) with a modern web-based reporting system that eliminates performance bottlenecks, supports concurrent multi-user access for the finance team, and meets current compliance obligations -- delivered within budget by end of Q3.' This reads as a feature list embedded in a sentence rather than an invariant purpose. It lists specific solutions (eliminate performance bottlenecks, support concurrent access, meet compliance) rather than expressing the core purpose (enable reliable financial reporting/decision-making). Including delivery timeline in the mission conflates project scope with purpose.

expectations.text: Goals include both quantified performance targets from the transcript: 15+ concurrent users AND <2 minute report runtime
expectations.evidence: G1: 'Reduce the monthly report generation time from the current ~45 minutes to under 2 minutes.' G2: 'Support at least 15 simultaneous users (full finance team) without degradation or crashes'. Both targets present. Note: the '~45 minutes' baseline appears nowhere in the transcript (Sarah says 20 minutes); this is a factual error but the assertion only checks that the targets are present.

expectations.text: Constraints capture both hard constraints stated explicitly: on-prem only AND $50k budget cap
expectations.evidence: C1: 'Total project cost must not exceed $50,000, including all infrastructure expenditures.' C2: 'The system must be hosted on-premises. Cloud hosting is prohibited for financial data per legal policy.' Both present.

expectations.text: Constraints or Premises capture that SAP and Salesforce integrations cannot be changed (IT constraint, not just a technical choice)
expectations.evidence: C3: 'SAP and Salesforce integrations cannot be altered. The new system must work with the existing data pipelines as-is.' P1: 'The system will continue to pull data from the existing SAP and Salesforce integrations. These integrations are fixed and cannot be modified (IT policy).' Correctly frames it as IT policy.

expectations.text: Audit trail requirement is included with its compliance motivation (not just listed as a feature) — the 'incident last year' context signals regulatory/compliance driver
expectations.evidence: C5: 'Every report run must produce an immutable audit log entry containing: user identity, timestamp, and report executed. This is a compliance-driven hard requirement.' G5: 'Log every report execution with the identity of the user who initiated it and a timestamp, satisfying compliance requirements identified after a prior incident.' The compliance motivation and 'prior incident' context are captured.

expectations.text: Premises are presented in a table format that includes a 'Risk if false' or 'Consequence if false' column assessing what breaks if each assumption is wrong
expectations.evidence: Premises are presented as subsections (P1 through P5) with narrative paragraphs, not in a table format. There is no 'Risk if false' or consequence assessment for any premise.

expectations.text: Output includes a Mission Space or Solution Space section that presents evaluated alternatives (e.g. COTS vs custom build, reporting platform options), domain context, or identified knowledge gaps
expectations.evidence: No Mission Space, Solution Space, or alternatives section. The output ends at section 4 (Constraints) with C6. No evaluated alternatives, domain context, or knowledge gaps are presented.

claims.evidence: The transcript says current reports 'take 20 minutes when they don't crash' (Sarah). The 45-minute figure appears nowhere in the transcript. This appears to be an error.
Sarah says 'eight years now' which from 2026 would be ~2018, not 2009. The '2009' claim is likely an error.

suggestions.reason: Both with_skill and without_skill outputs fabricate a '45-minute' baseline that doesn't appear in the transcript (actual: 20 minutes from Sarah). A factual accuracy assertion checking key numbers against the source would catch this.

eval_feedback.overall: Reasonable content extraction but missing structural elements (risk assessment, alternatives, mission quality). The mission reads as a project description rather than an invariant purpose statement.
